text.title: Lean Stack Bootstrapper Blueprint Worked Example
text.summary: End-to-end worked example applying lean-stack-bootstrapper-blueprint to a representative case. Walks step-by-step through the procedure, shows the produced artefact, and highlights the rules it satisfies.

section.title: Setup
section.p: Context: a team that matches the Applies-If conditions for lean-stack-bootstrapper-blueprint. Inputs are populated per the Prerequisites table.

list.item: Step 1: List the product's actual needs: hosting, DB, auth, payments, DNS, observability. — produced service-map.
Step 2: Apply r1: pick exactly one vendor per role (≤5 total). — produced service list.
Step 3: Set monthly ceiling + alert threshold per vendor admin UI. — produced ceiling configured.
Step 4: Author upgrade-trigger table (r5). — produced trigger doc.
Step 5: Diagram the architecture (1-page Markdown + mermaid). — produced stack-diagram.md.

section.title: Final artefact
section.p: The team commits the final artefact (per spec shape) at the version-controlled path declared in the output contract. Owner signs and links it to downstream consumers.

list.item: r1-five-services-max: Stack has ≤5 services: Vercel (hosting+edge), Supabase (DB+auth+storage), Stripe (payments), Cloudflare (DNS+CDN+email f
r2-cost-ceiling-explicit: Set a monthly cost ceiling ($50, $200, $500). Alert at 80%. Auto-pause non-essential at 100%.
r3-no-premature-docker: Do NOT introduce Docker, Kubernetes, or self-hosted services before the lean stack hits a real bottleneck.
r4-vendor-lock-acceptance: Accept vendor lock-in as a feature. Free tier's job is shipping, not portability. Optimize portability after PMF.
r5-upgrade-triggers-documented: Document triggers to graduate: monthly cost > 30% of MRR, p95 latency > 1s, vendor outage > 4h/month. Otherwise
